items.title: Live updates: ZachXBT calls hardware wallets complete garbage; BTC steady after Korea rate hike
items.description: Blockchain investigator ZachXBT came down heavily on hardware wallets. BTC traded steady near $65,000 after South Korea's rate hike.

items.title: U.S. Senate unanimously opposes clemency for FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried
items.description: The nonbinding resolution passed without objection after Bankman-Fried asked for clemency, months after Trump pardoned other major crypto figures, including Changpeng Zhao and Ross Ulbricht.

items.title: A bitcoin wallet dormant since the 2017 peak just moved $383 million
items.description: The coins went to a fresh address rather than an exchange, so nothing has been sold yet.

items.title: Ether outruns bitcoin as ETF money returns, almost all of from BlackRock's fund
items.description: This is not a broad rally as bitcoin is up 4% over the same stretch, and solana, TRON and hyperliquid are all lower.

items.title: Two groups of bitcoin investors sell on the rise as U.S. inflation lifts prices to nearly $65,000
items.description: Bitcoin surges toward $65,000 on softer-than-expected inflation data, but on-chain signals show two key investor groups selling into the bounce.

items.title: Coinbase's Jesse Pollak steps back from Base app leadership after admitting his crypto social strategy failed
items.description: Pollak said that Jordan Fish, also known on X as 'Cobie,' will lead the Base app team.

items.title: DTCC moves tokenized securities into live trading, marking a milestone for Wall Street's blockchain push
items.description: DTCC's first live production trades using tokenized securities show how blockchain could reshape the infrastructure behind Wall Street.

items.title: Cantor and Securitize collaborate on blockchain-based IPOs
items.description: The move creates a pathway for public companies to raise capital onchain and issue tokenized securities.

items.title: Hyperion DeFi to deploy 500K HYPE for Hyperliquid HIP-3 markets
items.description: The deal gives Hyperion an equity stake in Skew and a share of listing-service revenue as it expands the utility of its HYPE treasury assets.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Trump to meet with senators over CLARITY Act on Thursday: Politico
items.description: US President Donald Trump is set to meet with several senators on Thursday as negotiators race to get the CLARITY Act across the line before the Senate’s August recess.

items.title: Base’s social bet left it trailing in prediction markets and perps: Pollak
items.description: Base creator Jesse Pollak is stepping back from leadership of the Base App after admitting he was “definitively wrong” to bet on social experiences driving crypto adoption.

items.title: Stanford study says 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets enable settlement manipulation
items.description: Researchers found that Polymarket’s five-minute Bitcoin prediction markets create incentives to manipulate spot prices around contract settlement, proposing longer settlement windows as a potential fix.

items.title: Revolut receives in-principle approval from UAE authorities for crypto services
items.description: Dubai’s Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority approved the London-headquartered fintech company for broker-dealer, management and investment, and exchange services in the UAE.

items.title: US Senator blasts AG pick for ‘dismantling’ crypto unit, Trump’s CZ pardon
items.description: President Donald Trump’s pick for US Attorney General faced criticism from members of the Senate Judiciary Committee on his approach to crypto enforcement and the pardon of former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao.

items.title: Ostium pauses trading as security firms report multimillion-dollar oracle exploit
items.description: Ostium halted trading and advised revoking contract approvals after blockchain security firms reported an apparent oracle-related exploit of its OLP liquidity vault, with estimated losses ranging from $18 million to $22 million.

items.title: U.S. slaps 25% tariff on most Brazilian goods over 'unfair trade practices'
items.description: A separate U.S. probe into forced-labor enforcement could see an additional 12.5% duty on Brazilian goods on top of the 25%, with the decision due next week.

items.title: TSMC second-quarter profit jumps over 77%, beating estimates, on high-end chip boom
items.description: TSMC announced a second-quarter profit, following its June revenue figures released earlier this week.

items.title: SK Hynix shares plunge over 11% as Asia sees tech rout, tracking U.S. chip losses
items.description: Asian semiconductor stocks tumbled on Thursday as a selloff in U.S. chipmakers spilled into the region.

items.title: Alibaba and Baidu shares jump in Hong Kong on Apple AI partnership
items.description: The technological rivalry between China and the U.S. has intensified, as they race for AI dominance.

items.title: Fed Chairman Warsh says he meets 'often' with Trump administration, defends independence
items.description: Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that he speaks with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent frequently outside their regular weekly meetings.

items.title: Ex-Fed advisor gets over three years in prison for lying about China ties
items.description: The case marks one of the most prominent U.S. prosecutions alleging Chinese intelligence targeting of U.S. institutions, as Trump intensifies its pursuit of foreign economic espionage.

items.title: United earnings top estimates but airline expects $6 billion in added fuel costs
items.description: United reported higher revenue for premium, corporate and no-frills basic economy tickets and higher revenue for both domestic and international trips.

items.title: Bank of Korea raises rates to 2.75% in first hike in over three years
items.description: The 25 basis point hike by the Bank of Korea was in line with a survey of economists polled by Reuters.

items.title: Bitcoin tests $65,500 resistance as volume fades: Live levels
Bitcoin steady at $64.5k as markets parse cooling rate jitters, Iran tensions
Bitcoin turns marginally lower as soft U.S. Inflation data offset by Iran tensions

U.S., UK back cross-border stablecoin use in joint framework push
MyEtherWallet (MEW) Kicks off "Hot Stock Summer" with New Trade & Hold Tokenized Stock Activation
US will not win Iran war from the air, Trump’s ex-defence chief warns
items.description: Mark Esper tells FT more bombing will not change Tehran’s behaviour, as president vows to step up attacks

items.title: US hits tanker heading for Kharg Island under renewed Iran blockade
items.description: Military clashes over control of Strait of Hormuz threaten global oil supplies

items.title: Hungary’s regime change
items.description: What will the dismantling of the Orbán machine lead to?

items.title: The perks of parenting by spreadsheet
items.description: One simple trick to share it equally

items.title: Conflicts of interest are back — and more blatant than ever
items.description: Democrats would do well to focus their fire on the use of public office by Donald Trump’s circle for private gain

items.title: Energy IPOs surge as investors hunt for ways to play AI boom
items.description: Companies coming to market raise money at fastest pace this century, although many stocks perform poorly later

items.title: India Inc goes on a foreign buying spree
items.description: Outflows come as foreign investors flee country’s markets at fastest pace ever this year

items.title: Messi punishes England to drive Argentina into World Cup final
items.description: Reigning men’s champions celebrate comeback 2-1 win after opponents retreat into defensive shell

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=thr6MYyGr0s! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: AI is so big, it’s now impossible for investors to avoid
items.description: Exposure to the artificial-intelligence boom has become virtually impossible for investors to avoid. The theme hasn’t just cornered the stock market. It has systematically swallowed up corporate credit and venture capital, too.

items.title: United Airlines gets hit by a $6 billion added-fuel-cost headwind
items.description: Investors zero in on United’s potentially fatter jet-fuel bill — the airline said it expects nearly $6 billion in additional fuel expenses for the year.

items.title: The trucking market is tighter than it’s been in years — but investors are upbeat on J.B. Hunt
items.description: Shares of the trucking giant jump 8% after hours, as fuel charges and demand for its railroad-connection business lift results

items.title: ‘They cut off online access’: My mother’s bank balance plummeted after her death. What’s going on?
items.description: “Since her death, her bank balance fell to $9,000 from $16,000.”

items.title: ‘I don’t want to sound callous’: Why base your Social Security claim on life expectancy? We could die at any time.
items.description: “The actual date of death ultimately affects only the surviving beneficiaries.”

items.title: Your Netflix bill is up 29% in just over a year. It’s time for Washington to step in.
items.description: Netflix is still a Wall Street favorite — and a target for government regulators

items.title: My husband and I are retired. Our financial adviser, who is in his 30s, called us ‘you guyses.’ Is that unprofessional?
items.description: “I’m wondering whether I should ask to work with someone closer to our generation.”

items.title: You are missing the bond deal of the decade — and it is guaranteed to beat inflation
items.description: TIPS are offering a rare gift — and it is time to lock in a generous payout.

items.title: Silicon shadows: inside the black market for AI chips | FT Film

items.title: Is shock the new reality? | FT Rethink
items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OTGqVSmG3cw! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: UK economy grew 0.1% in May
items.description: Figure comes as Andy Burnham prepares to enter Number 10

items.title: Uber agrees €13bn deal to acquire Delivery Hero
items.description: German food delivery group will spin off Turkish and some European operations to New York-based SSW Partners

items.title: An unelucidating post about the UK’s mysterious ‘Other’ direct debits
items.description: Committing to debit

items.title: Merz and Macron to reset ties after fighter jet project collapse
items.description: Also in this newsletter: former Norway premier calls for EU membership debate and Orbán ex-minister joins China’s BYD

items.title: FirstFT: UK business struggles to contact Burnham
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Trump’s BBC lawsuit and energy IPOs

items.title: Trump administration slaps 25% tariff on Brazil as bilateral ties deteriorate
items.description: Levy is part of a deepening rift between Washington and Brasília ahead of South American presidential election

items.title: South Korea stocks slump after first rate rise in 3 years
items.description: Bank of Korea’s first move under Shin Hyun-song comes amid concern over persistent inflation and currency weakness

items.title: Will Stripe swipe PayPal?
items.description: The cutting-edge tech start-up is pursuing the older payments group

items.title: India Is Moving Fast to Build A.I. Data Centers. A Coastal City May Pay the Price.
items.description: With India lagging in the technology, officials are embracing giant data centers. But critics say the megaprojects will use up energy and water, without providing long-term jobs.

items.title: Europe Wants to Break Free From American and Chinese Technology. But How?
items.description: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.

items.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs on Brazil
items.description: The United States accused the country of unfair trade practices. The tariff will replace ones that the Supreme Court struck down.

items.title: New York Times Files Motion to Quash Subpoenas of Its Journalists
items.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel grand jury testimony as part of an investigation into Times reporting on the president’s new Air Force One jet.

items.title: NextEra and Dominion File to Form a Huge Power Company
items.description: They asked regulators in three states and at a federal agency to approve a deal that would create a combined company with 10 million customers across the Southeast.

items.title: PayPal Receives $53 Billion Takeover Offer Involving Stripe
items.description: PayPal, which owns Venmo, is the target of what one group of analysts described as a “lowball” offer from Stripe, another payment processor, and Advent, a private equity firm.

items.title: United Airlines Will Soon Let You Take Over the Middle Seat, for a Price
items.description: The option, set to go on sale on some flights later this year, will feature a shared table in the middle seat. The move may actually save the airline money.

items.title: Senators Grill Justice Dept. Officials on New York Times Subpoenas
items.description: Democrats questioned the acting attorney general, Todd Blanche, and a U.S. attorney about subpoenas issued to Times journalists who reported on security concerns with the president’s new plane.
